content: Each player checks their hand size at the end of the player phase, discarding down to or drawing up to their hand size value.

During the Deal Encounter Cards step of the villain phase, for each hazard icon on cards in play, deal one player one additional card (not one per player); additional cards are dealt in player order. Each hazard icon is equivalent to: 'During the Deal Encounter Cards step of the villain phase, deal 1 additional encounter card.

Additional cards from hazard icons are dealt in player order.'

If an ability heals a character, damage sustained can be removed. A heal effect can only bring a character to its maximum hit points unless it explicitly allows going above the maximum.

Effects that move damage off a character are considered to heal that character.

A card with the hinder X keyword enters play with X threat on it.

  • Equivalent to:
    This card enters play with X threat on it.
  • This threat is in addition to any threat it normally enters play with.

• Each character has a hit point value representing durability. 'Starting hit points' refers to an identity's printed value.
  • An identity's/villain's hit point dial represents remaining hit points; if reduced to zero, the player is defeated and eliminated, or that villain stage is defeated.
  • When an ability gives an identity/villain '+X hit points,' increase the dial by X (and reduce it by X when the effect ceases).
  • Damage on an ally/minion is tracked by damage tokens; zero or fewer remaining hit points defeats and discards it.
  • If a 'gets +X hit points' effect ceasing causes damage equal to/greater than hit points, that ally/minion is defeated.
  • Some characters may have infinite hit points and cannot be defeated by damage, though damage may still be dealt.
